---
title: "Draft: Results"
---

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)

# loading in data
r8 <- read_excel("case-and-demographic-characteristics-table-r8-2023-2024.xlsx", col_names = FALSE)
r100 <- read_excel("case-and-demographic-characteristics-table-r100-2023-2024.xlsx", col_names = FALSE)
table2 <- read_excel("fatal.xlsx", col_names = FALSE)

#filter for relevant rows
newr8 <- r8 %>%
  filter(if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("ffice|anufacturing|alls", ., ignore.case = TRUE)))

newr100 <- r100 %>%
  filter(if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("ffice|anufacturing|alls", ., ignore.case = TRUE)))

new_table2 <- table2 %>%
  filter(if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("ffice|anufacturing|alls", ., ignore.case = TRUE)))
```




## Results

Data Characteristics

Data was taken from two BLS sources covering 2023-2024 (SOII) and 2024 (CFOI). The SOII measured nonfatal injury rates per 10,000 full-time workers by industry and event type, while the CFOI provided a complete count of all fatal work injuries.

Primary Analysis

Nonfatal injury rates from the SOII showed that manufacturing workers had a rate of [X] per 10,000 FTE workers, compared to [X] for office/finance workers. Fall-specific rates were [X] for manufacturing and [X] for office workers, suggesting office workers [are/are not] at greater fall risk than manufacturing workers.

Secondary Analysis

Injury severity was measured using median days away from work. Manufacturing workers had a median of [X] days compared to [X] days for office workers, indicating injuries in [sector] tend to be more severe. Fatal injury data from the CFOI showed [X] fatal injuries in manufacturing compared to [X] in finance/office sectors in 2024, with falls accounting for [X] and [X] respectively.


Overall, the data suggest that manufacturing workers face [higher/lower] injury rates than office workers, however office workers may face [greater/lesser] fall-specific risk than expected. Injury severity as measured by days away from work was greater in [sector], and fatal injuries were more common in [sector].

still need to work with the data before running tests
